what: Method 1: a PSU bootstrap that re-applies the recipe
title: Variance estimation
text: bootstrap_weights() resamples primary sampling units (PSUs) replacement within strata re-runs recipe replicate. Pass inert recipe (call prep() first): bootstrap preps per replicate. multiplier Rao-Wu rescaling bootstrap. Consider stratum hh nhn_h PSUs, mhm_h drawn replacement (default mh=nh−1m_h = n_h - 1). Let thi*t_{hi}^{*} number times PSU ii selected replicate. Every unit PSU weight rescaled λhi=1−mhnh−1+mhnh−1nhmhthi*,\lambda_{hi} = 1 - \sqrt{\tfrac{m_h}{n_h - 1}}   + \sqrt{\tfrac{m_h}{n_h - 1}}\;\frac{n_h}{m_h}\,t_{hi}^{*}, replicate weight wi*=λhiwiw_i^{*} = \lambda_{hi}\,w_i. factor expectation one resampling, 𝔼(λhi)=1\mathbb{E}(\lambda_{hi}) = 1, keeps replicate design-unbiased, construction never turns negative, recipe can re-prepped every replicate without invalid weights. Whole PSUs kept together (every unit drawn PSU retained), design’s clustering requires.
code: dat <- sample_one dat$age_grp <- cut(dat$age, c(0, 30, 45, 60, Inf),                    labels = c("18-30", "31-45", "46-60", "60+"))  spec <- weighting_spec(dat, base_weights = pw) |>   step_unknown_eligibility(unknown = unknown_elig, by = "region") |>   step_drop_ineligible(ineligible = ineligible) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = hh_responded, method = "weighting_class",                    by = "region") |>   step_select_within(prob = p_within) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class",                    by = c("region", "sex", "age_grp")) |>   step_calibrate(method = "raking",                  margins = list(region = c(table(population$region)),                                 sex    = c(table(population$sex))))  boot <- bootstrap_weights(spec, replicates = 200, strata = "region",                           psu = "psu", seed = 2024, progress = FALSE) boot #> <weightflow bootstrap> #>   replicates : 200 #>   units      : 417 (active: 209) #>   strata     : region #>   psu        : psu

title: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
text: weighting recipe, black box. whole process — eligibility, selection, nonresponse, calibration, trimming — one explicit, auditable, pipeable object read top bottom. Flexible engines nonresponse outcome models. Response propensities model-calibration outcomes can fitted logistic regression, CART, random forest gradient boosting (xgboost) — API, swap one argument. Cross-fitting tame overfitting. Flexible learners can overfit propensity blow weights; optional k-fold cross-fitting estimates unit --sample, folds formed cluster leakage. Calibration controls extreme weights. Beyond raking, post-stratification GREG, ridge (penalized) calibration relaxes targets keep weights stable many auxiliaries. Principled trimming. usual far-fence, plus Potter’s MSE-optimal cutoff, chosen data instead hand. Recipe-aware variance. bootstrap re-applies every step replicate, standard errors carry variability whole cascade. Note. title: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
text: recipe inert: building computes nothing. prep() walks steps order estimates cascade factors; collect_weights() extracts final weights. Separating define apply makes whole process reproducible auditable, exactly lets bootstrap re-run entire cascade per replicate.
code: library(weightflow)  recipe <- weighting_spec(sample_one, base_weights = pw) |>   step_unknown_eligibility(unknown = unknown_elig, by = "region") |>   step_drop_ineligible(ineligible = ineligible) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = hh_responded, method = "weighting_class",                    by = "region") |>   step_select_within(prob = p_within) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "propensity",                    formula = ~ region + sex + age, engine = "logit",                    num_classes = 10) |>   step_calibrate(method = "raking",                  margins = list(region = c(table(population$region)),                                 sex    = c(table(population$sex)))) |>   step_trim_weights() |>   step_assert(max_deff = 3)  fitted <- prep(recipe)              # estimate the cascade summary(fitted)                     # per-stage diagnostics + Kish deff wts    <- collect_weights(fitted)   # data.frame with .weight

title: Calibration to population totals — step_calibrate
text: Adjusts weights weighted sample reproduces known population totals auxiliary variables, staying close possible input weights (Deville & Sarndal 1992). Supports raking (IPF categorical margins), post-stratification, linear/GREG calibration, optionally bounded (logit distance explicit bounds calibration factor). linear calibration, penalty enables ridge (penalized) calibration, relaxes targets control extreme weights many auxiliaries.

title: Calibration to population totals — step_calibrate
code: step_calibrate(   spec,   margins = NULL,   method = c("raking", "poststratify", "linear"),   formula = NULL,   totals = NULL,   cluster = NULL,   equal_within_cluster = FALSE,   calfun = c("linear", "logit"),   bounds = NULL,   maxit = 50L,   tol = 1e-06,   penalty = NULL )

title: Calibration to population totals — step_calibrate
text: spec weighting_spec. margins named list ("raking"/"poststratify"). element named numeric vector target totals per category. E.g.: list(sex = c(M = 5000, F = 5200), region = c(N = 3000, S = 7200)). method "raking" (IPF, categorical margins), "poststratify" (single categorical variable) "linear" (GREG / regression estimator; handles continuous categorical auxiliaries together). formula ("linear") auxiliary formula, e.g. ~ sex + income. Uses model.matrix; includes intercept unless write ~ 0 + ... totals ("linear") named numeric vector population totals, names matching model.matrix columns (including "(Intercept)" = N intercept). names match, error lists expected ones. cluster ("linear") name cluster id column (e.g. "household"), equal weights within cluster. equal_within_cluster ("linear") logical. TRUE, Lemaitre-Dufour (1987) integrative calibration: single weight per cluster. Requires cluster. Final weights equal within cluster provided incoming weight also uniform within cluster. calfun ("linear") distance function: "linear" (g = 1 + u) "logit" (bounded construction). "logit", bounds required. bounds ("linear") numeric c(L, U) L < 1 < U. Bounds calibration factor g (g-weights). "linear" truncates; "logit" enforced smoothly. Avoids extreme/negative weights without separate trimming step. maxit, tol convergence control raking bounded calibration. penalty ("linear", unbounded) NULL positive cost(s) ridge (penalized) calibration. positive scalar applies cost every constraint; named vector sets cost per constraint (matched model.matrix columns). cost scale-free: large value keeps constraint (near) exact, small value relaxes control extreme weights many auxiliaries. ridge achieved totals longer match targets exactly; diagnostics report deviation.

title: Calibration to population totals — step_calibrate
code: # Raking to population margins weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class", by = "region") |>   step_calibrate(method = "raking",                  margins = list(sex    = c(table(population$sex)),                                 region = c(table(population$region)))) |>   prep() #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. nonresponse (weighting class) #>   2. calibration (raking) #> Status  : estimated (prep) #>  #> Stage summary: #>                     stage n_active sum_wts cv_wts deff_kish n_eff #>                      base      467    4371  0.236     1.056   442 #>  stage_1_step_nonresponse      270    4371  0.144     1.021   265 #>    stage_2_step_calibrate      270    4495  0.211     1.045   258 #>  #> deff_kish = 1 + CV^2 (Kish design effect from unequal weighting); #> n_eff = n_active / deff_kish. Both worsen with each adjustment and #> improve with trimming. #>   # ridge (penalized) calibration: relaxes the targets to control extreme # weights; a smaller penalty relaxes more. Uses only base R. pop_tot <- c("(Intercept)" = nrow(population),              regionSouth = sum(population$region == "South"),              regionEast  = sum(population$region == "East"),              regionWest  = sum(population$region == "West"),              sexM        = sum(population$sex == "M")) weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class", by = "region") |>   step_calibrate(method = "linear", formula = ~ region + sex,                  totals = pop_tot, penalty = 1) |>   prep() #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. nonresponse (weighting class) #>   2. calibration (linear, ridge) #> Status  : estimated (prep) #>  #> Stage summary: #>                     stage n_active sum_wts cv_wts deff_kish n_eff #>                      base      467    4371  0.236     1.056   442 #>  stage_1_step_nonresponse      270    4371  0.144     1.021   265 #>    stage_2_step_calibrate      270    4438  0.159     1.025   263 #>  #> deff_kish = 1 + CV^2 (Kish design effect from unequal weighting); #> n_eff = n_active / deff_kish. Both worsen with each adjustment and #> improve with trimming. #>

title: Nonresponse adjustment — step_nonresponse
text: Inflates weights respondents represent nonrespondents, assumption response ignorable given information used. response propensity can estimated weighting classes (cells) model ("propensity"), engines ranging logistic regression machine learning (regression tree, random forest, gradient boosting). Optional K-fold cross-fitting estimates propensity --sample avoid overfitting flexible engines can introduce. adjustment can applied person , via cluster, household level.

title: Nonresponse adjustment — step_nonresponse
text: spec weighting_spec. respondent 0/1 dummy column (1 = responded) logical condition (unquoted) TRUE respondents. Eligible cases respondents treated nonresponse. method "weighting_class" (cells) "propensity" (predictive model). character. Adjustment cells method = "weighting_class". formula predictor formula (right-hand side ), e.g. ~ age + region, used method = "propensity". engine engine estimate propensity method = "propensity": "logit" (logistic regression, base R), "tree" (CART via package 'rpart'), "forest" (random forest via package 'ranger') "boost" (gradient boosting via package 'xgboost'). 'rpart', 'ranger' 'xgboost' optional: needed pick engine. num_classes integer NULL. Controls propensities used: integer forms many propensity classes (cell adjustment within class); NULL applies direct factor 1/p unit. cluster character NULL. given, adjustment done cluster (e.g. household) level whole-household nonresponse: household counts (uniform) weight; "weighting_class" redistribution responding nonresponding households within cells, "propensity" model fitted one row per household (household auxiliaries), predicting household response. resulting factor assigned every member; nonresponding households go zero. always, active units (weight > 0) take part, units already dropped (unknown eligibility, ineligible) excluded automatically. crossfit integer NULL. given (number folds K >= 2), propensity estimated K-fold cross-fitting: fold model trained folds used predict held-fold, unit's propensity comes model see . avoids overfitting flexible engines (forest, boost) can produce, otherwise inflate weights. Folds formed cluster given (correlated units stay together). NULL (default) fits predicts -sample. crossfit_seed integer NULL. Seed reproducible fold assignment crossfit used.

title: Automatic weight trimming (survey-style) — step_trim_weights
text: Caps weights [lower, upper] redistributes change among untrimmed units preserve total, mirroring survey::trimWeights(). default weight may fall 1, upper cap chosen automatic rule: Tukey far-fence (Q3 + 3*IQR) , method = "potter", Potter's MSE-optimal cutoff.

title: Automatic weight trimming (survey-style) — step_trim_weights
code: step_trim_weights(   spec,   lower = 1,   upper = NULL,   method = c("tukey", "potter"),   strict = TRUE,   maxit = 50L )

title: Automatic weight trimming (survey-style) — step_trim_weights
text: spec weighting_spec. lower numeric. Lower floor (default 1: weight 1). upper numeric NULL. Upper cap. NULL, cap chosen automatically method. method rule automatic cap upper = NULL: "tukey" (default, Q3 + 3*IQR far-fence) "potter" (Potter's MSE-optimal cutoff, grid candidate cutoffs minimizes estimate bias^2 + variance balances bias trimming variance extreme weights). Ignored upper supplied. strict logical. TRUE (default), iterate cap+redistribution weight outside [lower, upper] (like survey's strict = TRUE). FALSE, single pass (redistribution may push weights slightly past cap). maxit integer. Maximum iterations strict = TRUE.

title: weightflow 0.1.0
text: First release. dependency-free, pipeable API compute survey weights design base weights chain hierarchical adjustment stages. Build recipe lazily, estimate prep(), extract weights collect_weights().

title: weightflow 0.1.0
text: step_unknown_eligibility() — redistribute weight unknown-eligibility cases known ones (person- household-level via cluster). step_drop_ineligible() — zero --scope units. step_select_within() — within-household selection (unequal prob equal n_eligible). step_nonresponse() — weighting-class propensity adjustment, person household level (cluster). step_calibrate() — raking, post-stratification linear/GREG calibration, bounded (Deville-Särndal) integrative cluster options. step_model_calibration() — Wu-Sitter model calibration. step_trim(), step_trim_weights(), step_round(), step_rescale() — trimming, rounding rescaling. step_assert() — quality checkpoint (deff, weight ratio, effective n).

title: weightflow 0.1.0
text: summary(), plot() weight_factors() per-stage diagnostics. design_effect() Kish design effect effective sample size. report_weighting() builds self-contained HTML report pipeline diagram, variables used, per-stage summaries per-step visuals.
